title: handroanthus ochraceus var. aguaraguensis
intro: Handroanthus ochraceus var. aguaraguensis, also known as Ipe Roxo, is a variety of flowering plant in the family Bignoniaceae, native to the Amazon rainforest. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiparasitic activities. The health benefits associated with Handroanthus ochraceus var. aguaraguensis include its potential to treat fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as an antiseptic and astringent. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although further research is necessary to confirm its efficacy and safety.
